function.name: get_drug_info
function.description: Returns reference information for a single medication: brand names, drug class, indications, mechanism of action, common side effects, contraindications, and pregnancy category.

function.name: get_drug_dosing
function.description: Returns recommended dosing for a drug given indication and patient parameters. Includes route, frequency, max daily, and renal/hepatic adjustment notes when applicable.

renal_function_egfr.description: Estimated glomerular filtration rate in mL/min/1.73m^2. Use 60 or higher to indicate normal renal function.

function.name: get_drug_interactions
function.description: Returns the interaction profile between two drugs: severity classification, pharmacological mechanism, clinical recommendation, and evidence level.

function.name: get_lab_reference_range
function.description: Returns the laboratory reference range (low, high, units) for a given test, with age and sex modifiers when relevant.

function.name: get_lab_result
function.description: Returns a specific lab result for a patient (synthetic mock IDs only, never real PHI). Includes value, units, reference range, abnormal flag, and draw timestamp.

function.name: get_vital_signs_reference
function.description: Returns the normal range for a vital-sign parameter, accounting for age category (especially important for pediatric vitals which differ sharply by age).

patient_age_yrs.description: Patient age in years (use 0 for neonates).

function.name: get_icd_code
function.description: Look up an ICD-10-CM code. Use lookup_type='by_code' to retrieve the description for a known code; use 'by_description' to find the code matching a clinical description.

query.description: ICD-10-CM code (e.g., 'E11.9') or clinical description (e.g., 'type 2 diabetes mellitus without complications').

function.name: get_clinical_guideline
function.description: Returns the most recent published guideline summary for a given condition from a specified professional organization.

organization.description: Issuing organization. AHA=American Heart Association, ADA=American Diabetes Association, USPSTF=US Preventive Services Task Force, IDSA=Infectious Diseases Society of America, ACC=American College of Cardiology, AAP=American Academy of Pediatrics, ATS=American Thoracic Society, ACS=American Cancer Society.

function.name: get_medical_literature
function.description: Searches the medical literature index (PubMed-style) and returns matching papers with PMID, title, journal, year, authors, and abstract snippet.

query.description: Search query — keywords, study name, drug, condition, or specific PMID.

max_results.description: Maximum number of results to return.
